subject: English
title: The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom

questions.text: The story 'The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom' is a Japanese folk tale about:
options: A kind old couple and their pet dog
questions.explanation: It centers on a selfless couple and their loyal dog, Muko.

questions.text: What was the name of the couple's dog?
questions.explanation: Muko was treated like their own child by the childless couple.

questions.text: How did the kind old couple treat Muko?
options: They fed it fish and treated it with love
They used it for heavy work
questions.explanation: They were extremely kind and provided the best food for the dog.

questions.text: Muko led the old man to a place where he found:
questions.explanation: The dog scratched the ground, and when the man dug, he found a glinting pile of gold.

questions.text: What did the kind couple do with the gold they found?
options: They kept it all for themselves
They bought a piece of land, gave a feast, and helped the poor
questions.explanation: Their generosity grew along with their wealth.

questions.text: In the same village, there lived a ________ couple.
questions.explanation: They were the opposite of the kind couple and were jealous of their luck.

questions.text: What did the greedy neighbors do to Muko?
options: They dragged him to their garden to find gold
questions.explanation: They tried to force the dog to find treasure for them too.

questions.text: When Muko scratched the ground for the greedy couple, what did they find?
questions.explanation: Instead of treasure, they found a foul-smelling dead animal.

questions.text: How did the greedy couple react to finding the dead animal?
options: They killed the dog in anger
questions.explanation: In a fit of rage, the old man kicked and beat the dog to death.

questions.text: Where did the kind old man bury Muko?
questions.explanation: He built a small monument and placed flowers for his beloved pet.

questions.text: The spirit of the dog appeared in the old man's ________.
questions.explanation: The dog told the man to cut down the pine tree over his grave.

questions.text: What did the dog's spirit ask the man to make from the pine tree?
options: A mortar for rice pastry and a mill for bean sauce
questions.explanation: These items were meant to provide more blessings to the couple.

questions.text: What happened when the kind couple used the mortar and mill?
options: The rice turned into gold and the beans into gold coins
questions.explanation: The magic of the dog continued to help the kind couple.

questions.text: What did the greedy neighbors do after seeing the gold from the mill?
options: They borrowed the mortar and mill
They burned the kind couple's house
questions.explanation: They hoped to get the same gold by using the same tools.

questions.text: When the greedy couple used the borrowed mortar and mill, the food turned into:
questions.explanation: Again, their greed resulted in something foul and useless.

questions.text: In anger, the greedy couple ________ the mortar and mill.
questions.explanation: They destroyed the magical objects in their frustration.

questions.text: The dog's spirit appeared again and told the man to use the ________ to make withered trees bloom.
questions.explanation: This is the core miracle described in the title of the story.

questions.text: A 'Daimyo' in Japanese history refers to a:
questions.explanation: Daimyos were powerful landholding lords in medieval Japan.

questions.text: What kind of tree did the old man make bloom with the ashes?
questions.explanation: A sprinkle of ash turned the bare branches into a mass of pink flowers.

questions.text: The Daimyo was so pleased with the old man that he:
options: Gave him a reward and silk robes
questions.explanation: He invited the 'Old Man who Made Trees Bloom' to his castle.

questions.text: When the greedy neighbor tried to make trees bloom for the Daimyo, what happened?
options: The ash blew into the Daimyo's eyes and nose
questions.explanation: The ash did not work for him and only caused discomfort to the lord.

questions.text: What was the final fate of the greedy old man?
options: He was punished/beaten to death by the Daimyo's men
questions.explanation: His greed and attempts to trick the lord led to his downfall.

questions.text: The kind couple lived to a ________ old age.
questions.explanation: Their virtue was rewarded with a happy and long life.

questions.text: What does the word 'withered' mean?
questions.explanation: It refers to trees that appear dead or have lost their vitality.

questions.text: The story emphasizes that:
options: Kindness is rewarded and greed is punished
questions.explanation: It is a moral tale highlighting the consequences of one's actions.

questions.text: Why did the kind couple call the dog 'Muko'?
questions.explanation: They loved him as if he were their own child.

questions.text: The old man was a ________ by profession.
questions.explanation: He worked hard in his fields from morning until sunset.

questions.text: The word 'gleamed' in the story means:
questions.explanation: It describes the appearance of the gold under the dog's paws.

questions.text: The greedy old couple were 'stingy'. Stingy means:
questions.explanation: They were mean-spirited and did not like helping others.

questions.text: Muko's spirit helped the kind man ________ times after the dog's death.
questions.explanation: First through the mill/mortar, then through the ashes, and appearing in dreams.

questions.text: The Daimyo was traveling in a ________.
questions.explanation: In old Japan, lords were carried in grand palanquins.

questions.text: It was a custom that when a Daimyo passed by, people should:
questions.explanation: It was a sign of respect and submission to the great lord.

questions.text: Did the kind old man kneel down for the Daimyo?
questions.explanation: He was already in the cherry tree waiting to perform the miracle.

questions.text: How did the Daimyo feel about the blooming tree?
options: He thought it was a wonder
questions.explanation: He stopped his procession to witness the beautiful sight.

questions.text: The greedy neighbor thought the ash would work for him because:
options: He believed the ash itself was magic, regardless of the person
questions.explanation: He didn't understand that the miracle came from the goodness of the heart.

questions.text: What happened to the Daimyo's procession when the greedy man threw the ash?
options: The dust choked the Daimyo and his wife
questions.explanation: Instead of blooming, the ash just created a mess and ruined the dignity of the parade.

questions.text: The word 'covetous' is used to describe the neighbors. It means:
options: Having a strong desire for someone else's possessions
questions.explanation: They were jealous and wanted what the kind couple had.

questions.text: Muko showed his love for the old man by:
options: Putting his paws against his legs and motioning with his head
questions.explanation: The dog was trying to tell the man about the hidden treasure.

questions.text: The 'mortar' was made of ________ wood.
questions.explanation: The spirit of the dog specified the use of the pine tree wood.

questions.text: This story is a classic example of a ________.
questions.explanation: It uses magical elements to convey a moral lesson.
